annotations.label: Latitude

anchor.snippet: latitude is a geographic coordinate that specifies the north - south position of a point on the surface of the Earth

annotations.note: Mathlib has no geographic-coordinate notion of latitude on Earth.

annotations.label: Geodetic latitude (brief)

anchor.snippet: the geodetic latitude of a point is the angle formed between the vector perpendicular

annotations.note: No geodetic-latitude (normal-to-ellipsoid angle) definition exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geoid

anchor.snippet: the physical surface is modeled by the geoid

annotations.note: The geoid (Earth equipotential surface) is a physical/geodetic notion absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Meridians and longitude

anchor.snippet: Planes which contain the rotation axis intersect the surface at the meridians

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes neither meridians nor longitude on a rotating sphere.

annotations.label: Equator

anchor.snippet: The plane through the centre of the Earth and perpendicular to the rotation axis intersects the surface at a great circle called the Equator

annotations.note: Grep for `equator` in Mathlib returns nothing; no formal equator definition.

annotations.label: Parallels (spherical latitude)

anchor.snippet: Planes parallel to the equatorial plane intersect the surface in circles of constant latitude

annotations.note: No parallel-of-latitude construction is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spherical latitude terminology

anchor.snippet: often termed the spherical latitude, to avoid ambiguity with the geodetic latitude

annotations.note: Mathlib has no spherical-latitude terminology or definition.

annotations.label: Named parallels on Earth

anchor.snippet: Besides the equator, four other parallels are of significance

annotations.note: Named geographic parallels (tropics, polar circles) are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tropical and polar circle latitudes equal to obliquity

anchor.snippet: The latitude of the tropical circles is equal to i and the latitude of the polar circles is its complement

annotations.note: Earth's axial obliquity and its relation to tropic/polar latitudes are not formalized.

annotations.label: Newton's oblate ellipsoid theorem

anchor.snippet: he proved that a rotating self-gravitating fluid body in equilibrium takes the form of an oblate ellipsoid

annotations.note: Mathlib has no rotating self-gravitating fluid equilibrium theory.

annotations.label: Ellipsoid parameters (a, b, f, e)

anchor.snippet: The shape of an ellipsoid of revolution is determined by the shape of the ellipse which is rotated about its minor

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks an ellipsoid-of-revolution structure with semi-axes, flattening, and eccentricity.

annotations.label: WGS84 defining values

anchor.snippet: the defining values for the WGS84 ellipsoid, used by all GPS devices

annotations.note: WGS84 reference ellipsoid constants are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geodetic latitude

anchor.snippet: Geodetic latitude : the angle between the normal and the equatorial plane

annotations.note: Geodetic latitude as the normal-to-equatorial-plane angle is absent.

annotations.label: Geocentric latitude (intro)

anchor.snippet: Geocentric latitude (also known as spherical latitude , after the 3D polar angle ): the angle between the radius

annotations.note: No geocentric-latitude definition exists; Mathlib's spherical notions concern abstract spheres only.

annotations.label: Eiffel Tower coordinates

anchor.snippet: the centre of the Eiffel Tower has a geodetic latitude of 48° 51′ 29″ N

annotations.note: A specific geographic data point unrelated to Mathlib's scope.

annotations.label: Meridian distance on the sphere

anchor.snippet: the latitude ( ϕ ) is therefore equal to the angle subtended at the centre by the meridian arc from the equator to the point concerned

annotations.note: Meridian arc length on a sphere as a latitude function is not formalized.

annotations.label: Meridian distance integral on ellipsoid

anchor.snippet: the distance along a meridian from latitude ϕ to the equator is given by

annotations.note: The elliptic integral for meridian arc length on an ellipsoid is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quarter meridian distance

anchor.snippet: The quarter meridian distance from the equator to the pole is

annotations.note: Quarter-meridian (pole-to-equator) distance via complete elliptic integral is not formalized.

annotations.label: Small meridian arc length

anchor.snippet: The length of a small meridian arc is given by

annotations.note: Infinitesimal meridian arc formula on an ellipsoid is not formalized.

annotations.label: Six auxiliary latitudes

anchor.snippet: There are six auxiliary latitudes that have applications to special problems in geodesy

annotations.note: The geodetic auxiliary-latitudes family is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geocentric latitude (auxiliary)

anchor.snippet: The geocentric latitude is the angle between the equatorial plane and the radius from the centre to a point of interest

annotations.note: No geocentric-latitude auxiliary function exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Max geodetic-geocentric difference

anchor.snippet: the maximum difference of

annotations.note: Maximum difference between geodetic and geocentric latitudes requires absent geodesy infrastructure.

annotations.label: Parametric (reduced) latitude

anchor.snippet: The parametric latitude or reduced latitude , β , is defined by the radius drawn from the centre of the ellipsoid

annotations.note: Parametric (reduced) latitude is not formalized.

annotations.label: Rectifying latitude

anchor.snippet: The rectifying latitude , μ , is the meridian distance scaled so that its value at the poles is equal to 90 degrees

annotations.note: Rectifying latitude is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Authalic latitude

anchor.snippet: The authalic latitude (after the Greek for

annotations.note: Authalic (equal-area) latitude is not formalized.

annotations.label: Conformal latitude

anchor.snippet: The conformal latitude , χ , gives an angle-preserving

annotations.note: Conformal latitude is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isometric latitude

anchor.snippet: The isometric latitude , ψ , is used in the development of the ellipsoidal versions of the normal Mercator projection

annotations.note: Isometric latitude / Mercator projection on an ellipsoid is not formalized.

annotations.label: Ordering of auxiliary latitudes

anchor.snippet: in the Northern hemisphere (positive latitudes)

annotations.note: Ordering inequalities among auxiliary latitudes require their unformalized definitions.

annotations.label: First-order expansion of auxiliary latitudes

anchor.snippet: To first order in the flattening f , the auxiliary latitudes can be expressed as

annotations.note: Series expansions in flattening f for auxiliary latitudes are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geodetic coordinates

anchor.snippet: The geodetic coordinates P( ɸ , λ , h ) are the latitude and longitude of the point N on the ellipsoid

annotations.note: Geodetic (lat, lon, height) coordinates on an ellipsoid are not formalized.

annotations.label: Spherical polar coordinates

anchor.snippet: The geocentric latitude θ is the complement of the polar angle or colatitude θ′ in conventional spherical polar coordinates

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks a spherical polar coordinate chart (grep for `spherical` finds only sphere geometry, not coordinates).

annotations.label: Ellipsoidal-harmonic coordinates

anchor.snippet: The set ( u , β , λ ) define the ellipsoidal-harmonic coordinates

annotations.note: Ellipsoidal-harmonic coordinate system is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Astronomical latitude

anchor.snippet: Astronomical latitude ( Φ ) is the angle between the equatorial plane and the true vertical direction at a point on the surface

annotations.note: Astronomical latitude (plumb line vs equatorial plane) is a geodesy notion absent from Mathlib.
